rulings.Case Title: ANGELA JOHNSON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024, line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or Summary Adjudication. Defendant City and Conty of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Its alternative motion for summary adjudication is DENIED as to causes of action (COA) 1 and 3 and GRANTED as to COA 2. The tentative ruling in its entirety has been emailed to counsel. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDER XUE VS. HIGHFIELD HOSPITALITY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 31, 2024, line 2. PLAINTIFF ALEXANDER XUE'S Amended MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Xue declaration demonstrates that the parties did not meet and confer in compliance with CCP 435.5. The parties are ordered immediately to comply with the code and meet and confer. If still necessary, plaintiff may re-new his motion by January 21, 2025. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
